subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 8 — 2026-07-15 — Alaska's Critical Mineral Engine, and Its Unbuilt AI
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 8

2026-07-15 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-ny9qzg

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (3.28 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: Alaska's Critical Mineral Engine, and Its Unbuilt AI

2 · Paste the post copy

Alaska just won a decade-scale federal bet to find its critical minerals. The AI headlining it is one unbuilt sentence.

On July 14 the NSF named a University of Alaska Fairbanks coalition its Critical Mineral Accelerator Engine. It starts with 15 million dollars in hand, with up to 160 million over ten years if it clears milestones.

The deck covers what Alaska actually won, the 56 of 60 minerals the USGS calls essential, the money as a ladder not a check, and the single named site so far.

Funded but unspecified, is that a credible start or a headline running ahead of the work?

#CriticalMinerals #AlaskaPolicy #NSFEngines

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources for todays deck
National Science Foundation, NSF awards 12 new Regional Innovation Engines, https://www.nsf.gov/news/nsf-awards-12-new-regional-innovation-engines-fuel-research
UAF Geophysical Institute, NSF funds UAF-led critical minerals coalition, https://www.gi.alaska.edu/news/national-science-foundation-funds-uaf-led-critical-minerals-coalition
Every award we track, with the live milestones, lives at alaskaaihq.com
Close to this Engine? Add in a reply what the announcement still leaves out.

Editor's note

Weights sum to 1.10, so 8.78 sits on a ~11 scale between No.5 (8.54) and No.4 (8.90); revision count 1 keeps the iteration-ladder threshold at 8.3 (0-2 band). No hard fail, no 6.9 cap. Honesty guardrails all held on-slide (NANA partner not owner, biological methods not biomining, only 7 named minerals, single illustrative pin with no Ambler/Pebble, $160M always conditional, $135M-to-$2B fenced as program-wide). The deck's real ceiling is artwork depth and hero/type variety, not honesty or story.

Automation changes this run

None. The machine ran to spec; no upgrades were needed.

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11am Alaska time (the day signal is robust; the hour is weak).
  • Upload the PDF as a document post and set the document title to the one in this draft.
  • Paste the first-comment source block within about 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes; threaded author replies drive reach.
  • Consider cross-posting from a personal profile; personal carousels tend to out-reach company-page ones.
